qas.q: When are locally written instructions required for EOD and when are they not?
qas.a:
Required - Level 2 emergency response (RSP & Disposal)

Not Required - Level 1 emergency response (RSP & Disposal)

qas.q: Persons not normally part of an explosives operation but have duties that require their presence, such as QA, safety or inspection personnel
Non-essential personnel with limited access. Stop all operations when they are present
What is the form title for an "Explosive Facility License"?
What type of oil is useful for covering pyrotechnic, tracer, flare and similar mixtures?
Created when two different materials come in contact and then are separated again; this includes when the two materials are rubbed against each other
You should keep static grounding to under what resistance by using grounding rods for grounding aircraft?
What materials should not be worn in order to reduce static electricity?
Which weather conditions aid in the build up of static electricity?
qas.a:
Cold, Dry Climates

Cold, Windy Climates

qas.q: Display sites will be freshly mowed how soon before the explosives display?
How far away from the intentional detonation sites must crowd lines be when doing a detonation for an air show display
Overflight during any air show display detonation will be restricted to what distance?
Simulators and smoke producing munitions...What are the requirements for EOD to train people on using these items?
qas.a:
1.) Classroom instruction

2.) Written test

3.) Be qualified to handle, maintain and inspect them

4.) Train personnel annually

qas.q: When using simulators or smoke producing munitions, how far from from the initiation point must all combustibles be cleared?
Will be in effect 30 minutes prior to thunderstorms being within a five nautical mile radius of the predetermined location
Will be in effect whenever any lightning is occurring within a five nautical mile radius of the predetermined location
When supporting nuclear weapons, how far out must you receive lightning advisories during operations?
What type of storage is preferred for all types of explosives and mandatory for bulk HE, solid propellants and pyrotechnics?
AE received without an assigned hazard classification should be treated as what and stored how?

When warranted by operational considerations or magazine non-availability, and when safety is not compromised, mixed storage of limited quantities of some items from different groups is allowable. What is the NEWQD limit on this condition?
What compatibility group are WP munitions considered?
How must determine that an item that has failed to function is safe to ship?
What must Air Force civilian drivers have in order to transport explosives off base?
qas.a: Commercial Driver's License with hazardous materials endorsement

qas.q: When are EOD units authorized to transport minimum essential quantities of all HDs inside personnel compartments of vehicles?
qas.a: In an emergency response in a vehicle without a separate comparment

qas.q: Placards may be omitted for which HD on base?
How far from explosive laden buildings must you be in order to fuel a vehicle?
What must you do if you are to refuel a vehicle loaded with explosives?
qas.a: Maintain a bonded path between the tank being filled and tank being emptied and ground the entire system

qas.q: After loading a rail car with explosives...what should you do?
What does Set 1 of Chemical Hazards Symbol 1 indicate the presence of?
qas.a:
Highly toxic chemical agents

Blue background with Red border and dude

qas.q: What does Set 2 of Chemical Hazards Symbol 1 indicate the presence of?
qas.a:
Harassing Agents

Blue background with yellow border and dude

qas.q: What does Set 3 of Chemical Hazards Symbol 1 indicate the presence of?
qas.a:
WP or other Spontaneously Combustible Material

Blue background with white border and dude

qas.q: What does Chemical Hazards Symbol 2 indicate the presence of?
qas.a:
Incendiary or readily flammable chemical agents

Blue background with white border and mask

qas.q: What does Chemical Hazards Symbol 3 indicate the presence of?
qas.a:
Dangerous reaction will occur if water is used in an attempt to extinguish a fire

White background...red circle and diagonal...black fire and water bucket

qas.q: Fire withdrawal distance for non-essential personnel in an emergency situation
qas.a: Initial distance will be at least the IBD (Inhabited Building Distance) for the PES involved

qas.q: What is the minimum withdrawal distance for AE not involved in a fire in an emergency situation?
qas.a:
300-feet

125-feet for simulators and smoke producing devices

qas.q: How often should fire drills be conducted within the MSA?
How far around each PES should fire breaks extend?
Do not conduct controlled burning of vegetation within how what distance from any explosive location?
Stacks of combustible materials should keep at least 100-feet away from explosives and should not exceed what size?
How many and what type of fire extinguishers must be present at explosive sites and in vehicles transporting explosives?
How far from explosive locations must designated smoking areas be established?
What sizes do the fire division symbols come in?
What are the background and letter colors for the fire division symbols?
What are the colors for supplemental chemical symbols?
What are the four supplemental chemical symbols?
qas.a:
1.) G - G-Series Nerve Agents

2.) VX - VX Nerve Agents

3.) H - Mustard Agents

4.) L - Lewisite

qas.q: What are the firefighting actions to be taken with HD 1.1, HD 1.5, and Class IV liquid propellants?
qas.a: Do not fight fire unless rescue attempt in planned

qas.q: What are the firefighting actions to be taken with HD 1.2 and HD 1.6?
qas.a: Fight the fire at early stages and attempt to stop the spread

qas.q: What are the firefighting actions to be taken with HD 1.3?
qas.a:
May be fought if explosives are not directly involved

(Mostly using dry sand or dry powder

qas.q: What are the firefighting actions to be taken with HD 1.4?
Toxic agents without explosives components that normally would be assigned to HD 6.1 may be stored as which compatibility group?
Emergency withdrawal distance for non-essential personnel for unknown HD located in a facility, truck and/or tractor trailer
Emergency withdrawal distance for non-essential personnel for unknown HD located in a railcar
Emergency withdrawal distance for non-essential personnel for HD 1.1 and HD 1.5 during transportation
qas.a:
NEWQD ≤ 500-lbs ... 2,500 feet

NEWQD ≥ 500-lbs ... 5,000 feet railcars and 4,000 feet for other vehicles

Bombs and projectiles over 5-inches ... 4,000 feet

qas.q: Emergency withdrawal distance for non-essential personnel for HD 1.1 and HD 1.5 for facilities
Emergency withdrawal distance for non-essential personnel for HD 1.1 and HD 1.2 for transportation and facilities where MFD is greater than laid out in 91-201?
Emergency withdrawal distance for non-essential personnel for HD 1.2 and HD 1.6
qas.a:
Unknown Quantity - 2,500 ft

Known Quantity - 2,500 ft

qas.q: Emergency withdrawal distance for non-essential personnel for HD 1.3
qas.a:
Unknown Quantity - 600

Known Quantity - Twice the IBD with a 600 ft minimum

qas.q: Emergency withdrawal distance for non-essential personnel for HD 1.4
qas.a: Both known and unknown quantities - 300 ft

qas.q: What does ESMRM stand for?
qas.a: Explosive Safety and Munitions Risk Management

qas.q: What must be present at any location where explosives will be present or are projected to be present?
qas.a: An approved Explosive Safety Plan (ESP) or an Event Waiver

qas.q: What is the minimum standoff from the munitions stack when using concertainer barriers?
Quantities of fuel up to 500 gallons must be separated from PES's by what distance?
Quantities of fuel between 500 and 5,000 gallons must be separated fro PES's by what distance?
What can you not store with DoD AE?
qas.a: Capture Enemy Ammunition and Multinational AE

qas.q: What is the highest level of compliance for explosive safety as outlined in the International Ammunition Technical Guidelines (IATG)?
